guide.title: Vendor review workspace
guide.summary: Use the vendor review workspace to keep intake, questionnaire answers, evidence, linked records, and approval decisions together for one third-party review.
body: The vendor register stays lightweight and is mainly for browsing vendors, current status, and opening the active review.
The review workspace is where the real work happens: capture questionnaire responses, attach artifacts, link internal records, and move the review through approval states.

concepts.label: Vendor record
concepts.summary: A third-party register entry that identifies the vendor, service context, contact details, tier, and current lifecycle status.
concepts.why_it_exists: It separates vendor identity from one specific review cycle so the organization can keep long-term third-party history.
how_to_use: Create one vendor record per third party you need to assess or monitor.
Keep the tier, service summary, contact information, and current status up to date.

relations.summary: A vendor can have one or more review cycles over time.

concepts.label: Vendor review
concepts.summary: The operational review workspace for one vendor assessment cycle, including inherent risk, questionnaire answers, evidence, decision notes, and approval state.
concepts.why_it_exists: It gives the team one place to perform due diligence and record a clear decision before approval or reassessment.
how_to_use: Open the vendor review when you need to assess a new vendor or revisit an approved one.
Use it to collect answers, evidence, follow-up notes, and linked findings or risks.

relations.summary: Each review belongs to one vendor record.
Questionnaire items stay bound to the review that collected them.
A review can point to an existing risk record when the third party is part of a broader risk posture.
A review can link or lead to findings when due diligence identifies a concrete gap.

concepts.label: Vendor review profile
concepts.summary: A reusable review depth and cadence profile used to shape how one vendor review should be run.
concepts.why_it_exists: Review profiles let the team apply a consistent due diligence pattern for vendors with similar exposure, tier, or service characteristics.
how_to_use: Choose the review profile when creating or editing a vendor review.
Use the profile to drive the default questionnaire template and expected reassessment cadence.

relations.summary: A review can be assigned one profile to guide its depth and cadence.
Profiles can own one or more questionnaire templates.

concepts.label: Vendor questionnaire template
concepts.summary: A reusable question set linked to one review profile and copied into a concrete vendor review when applied.
concepts.why_it_exists: Templates keep recurring third-party reviews consistent without forcing every review to start from a blank questionnaire.
how_to_use: Select a questionnaire template for the review and apply it to create review-bound question items.
Use review-bound items for actual answers, status, and follow-up notes after the template has been copied.

relations.summary: Each template belongs to one review profile.
Applying a template creates review-bound questionnaire items.

concepts.label: Vendor questionnaire item
concepts.summary: One review-bound question and answer entry used to capture due diligence responses, their review status, and follow-up notes.
concepts.why_it_exists: It lets the team gather structured review answers before a full external questionnaire engine or template catalogue is introduced.
how_to_use: Add one item per question you need to track during the review.
Update the answer, response status, and follow-up notes as the review progresses.

relations.summary: Questionnaire items are stored inside one concrete vendor review.
Some question items are copied from an applied questionnaire template.

concepts.label: Vendor external review link
concepts.summary: A revocable, expirable external access link scoped to one vendor review.
concepts.why_it_exists: It allows controlled external collaboration without exposing the full internal workspace or unrelated records.
how_to_use: Issue an external review link only for the concrete review you want to share.
Choose whether the recipient may answer questionnaire items, upload evidence, or both.
Decide whether the link stays manual-only or whether the workspace should send the invitation through the organization SMTP connector.
Revoke the link when the collaboration window is over or when access should end immediately.

relations.summary: Each external link is scoped to one vendor review.
A link can allow the external participant to submit answers for review-bound questionnaire items.
A link can allow external evidence uploads into the review workspace.
Email invitation delivery for the link uses the organization outbound mail connector when enabled.
